meaning: n.抄写员,作者,划线器,(基督以前和同一时期的)犹太法学家vi.担任抄写员vt.用划线器划
examples: Some had to learn to write at school to become a scribe.
The Oscar-winning scribe was charged with vehicular manslaughter.
Journalism, as a veteran scribe once put it, is a rough old trade.
Patterson, 63, might seem like an unlikely scribe for the King James version of the Bible.
The new scribe is the poor, underpaid and underappreciated programmer who's cranking out Microsoft Word revisions.
